pi_items.source: Task description and metadata field match; this is the name of the view to be created
Official Chinook database schema (GitHub lerocha/chinook-database and SchemaSpy documentation); both tables confirmed to exist in the Chinook database with documented column structures
Task description specifies a view must use these SQL operations: COUNT for InvoiceCount, SUM for TotalSpent, GROUP BY for aggregation by customer fields

Official Chinook schema confirms Customer table has CustomerId, FirstName, LastName (for FullName concatenation), and Email fields. Invoice table has Total field (for TotalSpent). Column names match task description exactly
setup_libreoffice_base.sh line 65 copies the created ODB file to /home/ga/chinook.odb and sets it as the database path for tasks
install_libreoffice_base.sh lines 33-35 show the official download URLs for the Chinook database from the GitHub repository
install_libreoffice_base.sh line 63 converts Chinook SQLite to ODB format using create_chinook_odb.py; create_chinook_odb.py indicates HSQLDB 1.8 compatibility
Official Chinook database schema from SchemaSpy (schemaspy.org/samples/chinook/tables/Customer.html)
Official Chinook database schema from SchemaSpy (schemaspy.org/samples/chinook/tables/Invoice.html)

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a SQL view named 'CustomerPurchaseSummary' in the Chinook database (a well-known open-source sample database first released in 2009). The view must join the Customer and Invoice tables, include columns CustomerId, FullName (concatenated FirstName and LastName), Email, InvoiceCount (count of invoices), and TotalSpent (sum of invoice totals), with results grouped by customer. The database is stored in LibreOffice Base ODB format at /home/ga/chinook.odb, using embedded HSQLDB 1.8. The Chinook database contains synthetically generated customer and sales data (fictitious names, auto-generated sales information over a four-year period) along with real media metadata sourced from iTunes.
